---
title: "Figure 4 — R against IR: what conditioning on the interval buys, across the design space"
author: "Luciano Moffatt"
date: "`r Sys.Date()`"
output: html_document
---

<!--
THE COMPARATIVE MAP. Paper 1 asks what the likelihood must condition on; this figure answers it over
the design space instead of at the single cell Figure 2 measures. R is the literature anchor (the
published recursive filters treat each sample as instantaneous), IR is the answer the paper argues
for, and the two are put in adjacent rows so the comparison is a vertical glance.

Supersedes the split into an IR-only map plus a separate R-vs-IR map: the IR-only map's blocks were a
strict subset of this one's IR rows, so the two figures showed the same panels twice. IR's own
failure is still located here, in its own rows, with the same numbers.

PLANE: one panel per N_ch (columns), x = Delta*k_off (the 7 interval values swept inside every cell),
y = instrumental noise. ROWS are parameter (outer) then algorithm (inner) — algorithm innermost so
the R/IR pair is adjacent. Columns are spoken for by N_ch because N_ch is what the reader HAS: Delta
is a design decision and noise is instrumental, but the preparation gives you the channel count, so a
reader enters the figure by locating their own column.

BLOCKS:
  A  grouped MLE distributions, 3 design points x 2 algorithms. This is the referent: a distortion
     map is a colour field over a ratio of a score covariance to a Fisher information, and nobody has
     an intuition for 1.44. The cloud says it in the form a reader already knows — here is how your
     estimates scatter, here is what the algorithm said they would. R above IR at the same point.
  B  distortion-induced bias (first moment), from battery_sim_G at theta_sim.
  C  information distortion (second moment), from battery_pool_G at theta_pool.
First moment before second, the natural order.

TWO VERSIONS, both written on each knit:
  short  maps on k[off] only — the direction that carries the failure.
  long   maps on k[off] AND N[ch] — the two directions carry OPPOSITE signs, so any scalar averaged
         over parameters cancels them and reports a faithful algorithm. The long version is what
         makes that verifiable.
Block A is identical in both (an ellipse needs two axes, so it stays on the (k_off, N_ch) pair even
when the maps show one).

COVERAGE, and why the design points moved (verified 2026-07-22, do not re-derive):
  Figure 5's points sat at noise 0.05 and 0.5. macro_R exists at NEITHER: on the Gaussian anchor R
  has noise {0.1, 1, 10} plus an N_ch-scaled level, at N_ch {10, 100, 1000, 10000}, and its finer
  N_ch values (5, 20, 50) exist only at noise 0.1. IR has {0.05, 0.1, 0.2, 0.5, 1, 10} on a much
  finer N_ch grid. The intersection is what this figure plots: 4 decades x 3 noise levels. The
  points therefore moved to noise 0.1, N_ch 10 (1 and 2) and N_ch 10000 (3), where both algorithms
  have a cloud at group_size 100 over all 7 intervals.
  NOTE the noise axis is RAW instrumental noise, not noise/N_ch. Gating variance grows with N_ch, so
  the same raw level means a different instrumental fraction in each column and the rows are NOT
  vertically comparable across columns. That is a deliberate limit of this version: on the relative
  axis the existing cells only cross the transition at N_ch 10 (r = noise/N_ch reaches 1 and 10 there
  and nowhere else), so "does noise cure R" cannot yet be asked in the other three columns. The cells
  that would fix it are listed in papers/1_method/figures_build_plan.md.

TWO DIRECTORIES, one search path. R's noise 1 and 10 live in 87889e6 while everything else is in
1c2ae6f. Sanctioned by D-0: every CSV stamps its own engine hash in row 1, so the folder is a scratch
grouping and the file is the provenance record. Same design as figure_2.Rmd's DATA_DIRS.

TRAP: the "noise" label is not the physical noise. current_noise = label/1000, and the CSV column
noise_in_conductance_tau stores the LABEL. The y axis below is the label, and the caption says so.

TRAP: group_size 10 at few channels does not merely add noise, it REVERSES the sign of the N_ch
direction (its finite-sample inflation is comparable to the true deflation). ELL_GROUP = 100 is the
floor, at the cost of 100 fits and 14% relative uncertainty on a variance.
-->

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE, warning = FALSE, message = FALSE)
library(tidyverse); library(patchwork)
```

```{r params}
# battery-fed notebook, so the keys are the PREFIXED form that lands in the CSV `algorithm` column
ALGOS    <- c("macro_R", "macro_IR")          # order = row order inside each parameter block
ALGO_LAB <- c(macro_R = "R", macro_IR = "IR")

NCHS <- c(10, 100, 1000, 10000)               # the four decades: the intersection with what R has
DATA_DIRS <- c("../data/1c2ae6f", "../data/87889e6")   # search path, first hit wins

# THE y AXIS IS RAW INSTRUMENTAL NOISE, and dividing it by N_ch would be a mistake. Gating variance
# grows with N_ch, so the crossover to calibration is expected at an absolute noise PROPORTIONAL to
# N_ch; on a shared raw axis that renders as a staircase climbing column by column, and the
# staircase's slope in log-log IS the exponent. Normalising to noise/N_ch flattens it by construction
# and assumes the very relation this figure can measure. Raw axis, and let the triangle speak.
#
# THE GRID IS DECLARED, NOT A PRODUCT, because tracking that staircase makes it ragged: a higher N_ch
# needs a higher absolute noise to reach the same regime, so the swept levels differ per column.
# Declaring the pairs keeps the missing-cell hard stop working on a ragged grid - an absent file is
# still an error, never a silently shortened axis.
GRID_BASE <- tibble::tribble(
  ~nch,  ~z,
  10,    "0.1",  10,    "1",  10,    "10",
  100,   "0.1",  100,   "1",  100,   "10",
  1000,  "0.1",  1000,  "1",  1000,  "10",
  10000, "0.1",  10000, "1",  10000, "10")
# PENDING: the diagonal that makes the staircase visible, at constant instrumental/gating ratio
# r = noise/N_ch. TARGETED AT r = 0.01, 0.1 and 1, NOT 0.1/1/10, and that retargeting is a
# measurement, not a guess (figure_4_diag_nsim1000.Rmd):
#   - r = 10 is already answered and lies PAST the transition. The 82b956f probe measures R at
#     1.02-1.06 there in all four decades, and it is not underpowered: its bootstrap halfwidth is
#     0.04 in log10 against the 0.158 that R's noise-0.1 distortion of 1.44 would need, so a
#     departure that size would have been seen. Running r = 10 at nsim 10000 would buy a confirmation
#     of something already established.
#   - the crossover sits between r = 0.01 and r = 0.1, from the nsim 10000 noise-10 column read as a
#     function of r: 1.01 at r = 1 (N_ch 10), 1.05 at r = 0.1 (100), 1.24 at r = 0.01 (1000),
#     1.18 at r = 0.001 (10000).
# Six cells per algorithm, the rest of the diagonal already exists. Uncomment each row as its cell
# lands; nothing else needs editing, the axis range and its breaks are taken from this table.
#   dispatch: NCHS="100 1000 1000 10000 10000 10000"
#             N_NOISE="100 100 1000 100 1000 10000"
#             GROUP_SIZE="10 100"  N_ALGO="macro_R macro_IR"
GRID_PENDING <- tibble::tribble(
  ~nch,  ~z)
#   100,   "100",                                    # r = 1
#   1000,  "100",   1000,  "1000",                   # r = 0.1, 1
#   10000, "100",   10000, "1000",  10000, "10000")  # r = 0.01, 0.1, 1
GRID <- bind_rows(GRID_BASE, GRID_PENDING)

PIDX      <- c(on = 0, off = 1, unitary_current = 2, Current_Noise = 3, Num_ch_mean = 5)
COMPS <- c(total  = "Probit_statistics_Likelihood_Gaussian_Information_Distortion",
           sample = "Probit_statistics_Gaussian_Sample_Distortion",
           corr   = "Probit_statistics_Likelihood_Correlation_Distortion")

# the two parameter sets the two versions plot. k_off inflates, N_ch deflates: opposite signs.
PARAM_SHORT <- c("off")
PARAM_LONG  <- c("off", "Num_ch_mean")
PARAM_MATH  <- c(off = "log[10]~k[off]", Num_ch_mean = "log[10]~N[ch]")

RAMP <- c("#021024","#053061","#2166AC","#4393C3","#92C5DE","#D1E5F0","#F7F7F7",
          "#FDDBC7","#F4A582","#D6604D","#B2182B","#67001F","#3d0011")
bandcols <- function(breaks) {
  n <- length(breaks) - 1; ctr <- findInterval(0, breaks); nb <- ctr - 1; nr <- n - ctr; K <- max(nb, nr, 1)
  blue <- colorRampPalette(rev(RAMP[1:7]), space = "Lab")(K + 1)
  red  <- colorRampPalette(RAMP[7:13],     space = "Lab")(K + 1)
  cols <- rep(RAMP[7], n)
  if (nb > 0) cols[seq(ctr - 1, 1)] <- blue[2:(nb + 1)]
  if (nr > 0) cols[seq(ctr + 1, n)] <- red[2:(nr + 1)]
  cols
}
fmt_big <- function(x) formatC(x, format = "g", digits = 3)
DIST_LIN    <- c(0.5, 0.67, 0.8, 0.87, 0.91, 0.94, 0.97, 0.99, 1.01, 1.03, 1.06, 1.1, 1.15, 1.25, 1.5, 1.7, 2.5)
DIST_BREAKS <- log10(DIST_LIN)
DIST_PAL    <- bandcols(DIST_BREAKS)
DIST_LAB    <- fmt_big(DIST_LIN)

# Figure 5's bias scale was set from IR alone (-0.004 to +0.064) and R does not fit in it: R's N_ch
# bias runs to +0.29. Range measured, not guessed: excluding the three cells named below, the
# CI-shrunk bias spans -0.033 to +0.288, so +-0.3 clips nothing. Bands are log-spaced because IR's
# values (0.003 to 0.042) and R's (0.13 to 0.15) differ by more than a decade and a linear scale
# would put every IR cell in the neutral band.
#
# THREE CELLS ARE OFF ANY SCALE and are clamped: macro_R, N_ch 10000, Delta*k_off = 1, at all three
# noise levels, where the predicted N_ch bias is -2.78 log10 (a factor of 600) with a bootstrap
# interval of width 0.04. This is NOT a bootstrap artifact and NOT R-specific ill-conditioning: the
# Gaussian Fisher covariance at that cell has kappa = 75272 for R and 99459 for IR, so IR is worse
# conditioned there and its predicted bias is ~0. The reading: the bias is H^-1 times a
# miscalibration, IR's numerator is ~0 so nothing is amplified, R's is not and gets amplified along
# the direction the data barely constrains. The SIGN and the existence are meaningful; the MAGNITUDE
# is not a physical bias prediction, because a displacement along a near-null direction costs almost
# no likelihood. OPEN: whether those cells should be greyed as unidentified rather than clamped, and
# whether block B should plot bias/SE instead of bias. Decide before this figure is called final.
BIAS_LIN    <- c(-0.3, -0.15, -0.07, -0.03, -0.01, -0.003, 0.003, 0.01, 0.03, 0.07, 0.15, 0.3)
BIAS_PAL    <- bandcols(BIAS_LIN)
BIAS_LAB    <- formatC(BIAS_LIN, format = "g", digits = 2)
```

```{r data}
# search path, not a fixed directory: R's noise 1 and 10 are in a different run folder from
# everything else. A missing file is a hard stop, never a silently shortened axis.
findf <- function(nch, algo, z, kind) {
  f <- file.path(DATA_DIRS,
                 sprintf("figure_3_G_nch_%d_nsim_10000_%s_noise_%s_%s.csv", nch, algo, z, kind))
  hit <- f[file.exists(f)]
  if (!length(hit)) stop("figure_4: no file for ", algo, " N_ch ", nch, " noise ", z, " (", kind, ")")
  hit[1]
}

cells <- expand_grid(algo = ALGOS, i = seq_len(nrow(GRID))) %>%
  mutate(nch = GRID$nch[i], z = GRID$z[i]) %>% select(algo, nch, z)
cat("figure_4:", nrow(cells), "cells =", length(ALGOS), "algorithms x", nrow(GRID),
    "(N_ch, noise) pairs\n")
cat("  noise levels per N_ch (the staircase; a ragged row here is the point, not a defect):\n")
print(as.data.frame(GRID %>% group_by(nch) %>%
  summarise(noise = paste(sort(as.numeric(z)), collapse = " "), .groups = "drop")), row.names = FALSE)
for (a in ALGOS) {
  d <- unique(dirname(pmap_chr(filter(cells, algo == a), function(algo, nch, z)
        findf(nch, algo, z, "battery_pool_G"))))
  cat("  ", a, "resolved from:", paste(basename(d), collapse = " + "), "\n")
}

# one long-format reader for both anchors; `algorithm` is carried explicitly rather than trusted from
# the CSV column, because the row identity here comes from the filename we asked for.
readcell <- function(kind) pmap_dfr(cells, function(algo, nch, z)
  read.csv(findf(nch, algo, z, kind), skip = 1, stringsAsFactors = FALSE) %>%
    mutate(noise = z, algo = algo))

# BIAS comes from battery_sim_G, anchored at theta_sim. It cannot come from the pool files: a bias
# evaluated at the optimum is zero by construction because the score vanishes there.
bias <- readcell("battery_sim_G") %>%
  filter(component_path == "Probit_statistics_Gaussian_Distortion_Induced_Bias",
         param_index %in% PIDX[PARAM_LONG],
         statistic == "value", probit %in% c("mean", "quantile")) %>%
  mutate(key = case_when(probit == "mean" ~ "m", quantile_level == 0.025 ~ "lo",
                         quantile_level == 0.975 ~ "hi", TRUE ~ NA_character_)) %>%
  filter(!is.na(key)) %>%
  group_by(algo, noise, Num_ch, interval_in_tau, param_index, key) %>%
  summarise(v = mean(value), .groups = "drop") %>%
  pivot_wider(names_from = key, values_from = v) %>%
  # CI-aware toward ZERO: a cell whose interval covers 0 renders white
  mutate(Bconf = ifelse(m > 0, pmax(0, lo), pmin(0, hi)),
         lx = log10(interval_in_tau), ly = log10(as.numeric(noise)),
         param = names(PIDX)[match(param_index, PIDX)],
         nchf  = factor(paste0("N[ch]==", Num_ch), levels = paste0("N[ch]==", NCHS)))

stat <- readcell("battery_pool_G") %>%
  filter(component_path %in% COMPS, param_index == param_col, param_index %in% PIDX[PARAM_LONG],
         statistic == "value", probit %in% c("mean", "quantile")) %>%
  mutate(comp = names(COMPS)[match(component_path, COMPS)],
         key  = case_when(probit == "mean" ~ "m", quantile_level == 0.025 ~ "lo",
                          quantile_level == 0.975 ~ "hi", TRUE ~ NA_character_)) %>%
  filter(!is.na(key)) %>%
  group_by(algo, comp, noise, Num_ch, interval_in_tau, param_index, key) %>%
  summarise(v = mean(value), .groups = "drop") %>%
  pivot_wider(names_from = key, values_from = v) %>%
  # CI-aware: a cell whose 95% interval brackets 1 collapses to exactly 1 and renders white
  mutate(Dconf = ifelse(m > 1, pmax(1, lo), pmin(1, hi)),
         lx = log10(interval_in_tau), ly = log10(as.numeric(noise)),
         param = names(PIDX)[match(param_index, PIDX)],
         nchf  = factor(paste0("N[ch]==", Num_ch), levels = paste0("N[ch]==", NCHS)))

# UNIDENTIFIED cells: greyed, not clamped. Flag a cell where the value is off the plotted scale AND
# the Gaussian Fisher covariance is ill-conditioned (condition number > 3e4). kappa alone grows with
# N_ch for everyone and would grey the whole 10000 column; the clamp AND the high kappa together
# isolate the R corner (N_ch 10000, Delta = 1), a displacement along a near-null direction whose
# magnitude is not a meaningful bias/distortion. A distinct darker grey with a border, so it is not
# read as an unrun (grey85) cell.
kap <- readcell("battery_pool_G") %>%
  filter(component_path == "Probit_statistics_Spectrum_Condition_Number_Gaussian_Fisher_Covariance",
         statistic == "value", probit == "mean") %>%
  group_by(algo, Num_ch, noise, interval_in_tau) %>% summarise(kappa = mean(value), .groups = "drop")
KAPPA_MAX <- 3e4
bias <- bias %>% left_join(kap, by = c("algo", "Num_ch", "noise", "interval_in_tau")) %>%
  mutate(unident = (m < min(BIAS_LIN) | m > max(BIAS_LIN)) & is.finite(kappa) & kappa > KAPPA_MAX)
stat <- stat %>% left_join(kap, by = c("algo", "Num_ch", "noise", "interval_in_tau")) %>%
  mutate(unident = (m < min(DIST_LIN) | m > max(DIST_LIN)) & is.finite(kappa) & kappa > KAPPA_MAX)
cat("greyed (unidentified): bias", sum(bias$unident), "cells, distortion",
    sum(stat$unident[stat$comp == "total"]), "cells\n")

# NO SILENT CAPS. Both maps clamp to their scale, so report what the clamp swallowed. A non-zero
# count here means the scale is lying about the size of a departure, not that the figure is fine.
sat <- function(v, lo, hi, what) {
  n <- sum(is.finite(v)); b <- sum(v < lo, na.rm = TRUE); t <- sum(v > hi, na.rm = TRUE)
  cat(sprintf("  %-28s %d/%d below %.3g, %d/%d above %.3g\n", what, b, n, lo, t, n, hi))
}
cat("scale saturation (0 is the only acceptable answer for a final render):\n")
sat(log10(stat$Dconf[stat$comp == "total"]), min(DIST_BREAKS), max(DIST_BREAKS), "distortion")
sat(bias$Bconf, min(BIAS_LIN), max(BIAS_LIN), "bias")
```

```{r points}
# THREE design points, and the middle one is a control, not decoration. At the failing point two
# things act at once: the algorithm's distortion and the estimator's own finite-sample departure from
# normality. Point 2 holds group size, N_ch and noise FIXED and moves only the interval, so the
# difference between 1 and 2 is the algorithm alone. Point 3 is a calibrated design point, for scale.
#
# MOVED from Figure 5's points (N_ch 20 at noise 0.05, N_ch 10000 at noise 0.5): macro_R exists at
# neither noise level, and N_ch 20 is no longer a map column. These three sit on the noise-0.1 row of
# every map panel and on columns that exist for both algorithms.
ELL_POINTS <- tibble::tribble(
  ~lab, ~nch,   ~noise, ~dlt,  ~what,
  "1",  10,     "0.1",  0.01,  "distorted",
  "2",  10,     "0.1",  1,     "biased",
  "3",  10000,  "0.1",  0.1,   "faithful")
ELL_GROUP <- 100
```

```{r ellipses, fig.width = 7, fig.height = 4.8}
# GROUP SIZE 100, NOT 10. Each MLE is fitted to `group_size` recordings; at 10 the estimator carries a
# finite-sample variance inflation that belongs to the group size, not to the algorithm, and at few
# channels that inflation REVERSES the sign of the N_ch direction. The MAPS fit no estimates at all
# and are untouched by this.
PAIR <- c(off = "off", nch = "Num_ch_mean")   # the two opposite-signed directions
ell  <- function(S, cx, cy, n = 200) {        # 95% ellipse, 2 dof
  if (any(!is.finite(S))) return(NULL)
  e <- eigen(S, symmetric = TRUE); if (any(e$values <= 0)) return(NULL)
  th <- seq(0, 2 * pi, length.out = n)
  P <- e$vectors %*% diag(sqrt(e$values * qchisq(0.95, 2))) %*% rbind(cos(th), sin(th))
  tibble(x = cx + P[1, ], y = cy + P[2, ])
}

panel <- function(i, algo) {
  pt   <- ELL_POINTS[i, ]
  fcl  <- findf(pt$nch, algo, pt$noise, "mle_cloud_runs")
  fbat <- findf(pt$nch, algo, pt$noise, "battery_pool_G")
  fsim <- findf(pt$nch, algo, pt$noise, "battery_sim_G")

  pts <- read.csv(fcl, skip = 1) %>%
    filter(component_path == "Model_Parameters_Hat", statistic == "value",
           group_size == ELL_GROUP, param_name %in% PAIR,
           abs(interval_in_tau - pt$dlt) < 1e-9) %>%
    select(sample_index, param_name, value) %>%
    pivot_wider(names_from = param_name, values_from = value)
  bat <- read.csv(fbat, skip = 1) %>%
    filter(statistic == "value", probit == "mean", abs(interval_in_tau - pt$dlt) < 1e-9,
           param_index %in% PIDX[PAIR], param_col %in% PIDX[PAIR])
  cov2 <- function(cp) {
    d <- bat %>% filter(component_path == cp); m <- matrix(NA_real_, 2, 2); id <- unname(PIDX[PAIR])
    for (a in 1:2) for (b in 1:2) { v <- d$value[d$param_index == id[a] & d$param_col == id[b]]
                                    if (length(v) == 1) m[a, b] <- v }
    m / ELL_GROUP
  }
  cx <- mean(pts$off, na.rm = TRUE); cy <- mean(pts$Num_ch_mean, na.rm = TRUE)
  E  <- cov(cbind(pts$off, pts$Num_ch_mean), use = "complete.obs")
  src <- bind_rows(
    mutate(ell(E, cx, cy), source = "empirical"),
    mutate(ell(cov2("Probit_statistics_Gaussian_Fisher_Covariance"), cx, cy), source = "Fisher"),
    mutate(ell(cov2("Probit_statistics_Gaussian_Distortion_Corrected_Covariance"), cx, cy),
           source = "corrected")) %>%
    mutate(source = factor(source, levels = c("empirical", "Fisher", "corrected")))
  A <- function(S) { e <- eigen(S, symmetric = TRUE)$values
                     if (any(!is.finite(S)) || any(e <= 0)) NA_real_ else sqrt(prod(e)) }
  Fm <- cov2("Probit_statistics_Gaussian_Fisher_Covariance")
  rF <- A(E) / A(Fm)
  rC <- A(E) / A(cov2("Probit_statistics_Gaussian_Distortion_Corrected_Covariance"))
  # PER-AXIS ratios, not one area. An area multiplies an inflated direction by a deflated one and
  # hides both, which is the very cancellation the maps exist to expose.
  rx <- E[1, 1] / Fm[1, 1]      # k_off, the x axis
  ry <- E[2, 2] / Fm[2, 2]      # N_ch,  the y axis
  nf <- nrow(pts)
  shrink <- function(r) { lo <- r * (nf - 1) / qchisq(.975, nf - 1)
                          hi <- r * (nf - 1) / qchisq(.025, nf - 1)
                          if (r > 1) max(1, lo) else min(1, hi) }
  bandcol <- function(r) { sr <- shrink(r)
    if (isTRUE(all.equal(sr, 1))) "grey45"
    else DIST_PAL[max(1, min(length(DIST_PAL),
           findInterval(log10(sr), DIST_BREAKS, rightmost.closed = TRUE)))] }

  # THE ELLIPSE AND THE MAP ARE DIFFERENT OBJECTS, and the panel says so instead of leaving the
  # reader to discover it. The ellipse compares Cov(theta_hat) against the sandwich MARGINALISED over
  # the other parameters; the maps compare score against Fisher unmarginalised. The marginal reading
  # is systematically milder. Both numbers are printed: the multiplier from the cloud, and the map's
  # own value at the same cell, so a disagreement is visible as a stated fact.
  mval <- function(p) { v <- stat$Dconf[stat$comp == "total" & stat$algo == algo &
                                        stat$Num_ch == pt$nch & stat$noise == pt$noise &
                                        abs(stat$interval_in_tau - pt$dlt) < 1e-9 & stat$param == p]
                        if (length(v) == 1) v else NA_real_ }
  mx <- mval("off"); my <- mval("Num_ch_mean")

  # Figure 2's marker grammar: cross = truth, filled circle = empirical mean, open circle = truth plus
  # the bias the distortion theory PREDICTS (anchored at theta_sim, so a prediction and not a
  # re-description of the cloud).
  dib <- read.csv(fsim, skip = 1) %>%
    filter(component_path == "Probit_statistics_Gaussian_Distortion_Induced_Bias",
           statistic == "value", probit == "mean", abs(interval_in_tau - pt$dlt) < 1e-9,
           param_index %in% PIDX[PAIR]) %>%
    select(param_index, value)
  gx <- 2; gy <- log10(pt$nch)     # truth: log10(k_off) = log10(100) = 2
  gv <- function(nm) { v <- dib$value[dib$param_index == PIDX[[PAIR[[nm]]]]]
                       if (length(v) == 1) v else NA_real_ }
  mk <- tibble(x = c(gx, cx, gx + gv("off")), y = c(gy, cy, gy + gv("nch")),
               kind = factor(c("truth", "empirical mean", "predicted (truth + bias)"),
                             levels = c("truth", "empirical mean", "predicted (truth + bias)")))

  # symmetric window CENTRED ON THE TRUTH, so a displacement reads as a displacement. The window is
  # computed over BOTH algorithms at this point (below) so the R and IR panels of a column share it
  # and their clouds can be compared by eye rather than by reading the axis numbers.
  hx <- 1.10 * max(abs(c(src$x, pts$off, mk$x) - gx), na.rm = TRUE)
  hy <- 1.34 * max(abs(c(src$y, pts$Num_ch_mean, mk$y) - gy), na.rm = TRUE)

  cat(sprintf(paste0("fig4A %-3s point %s (%s): N_ch %d, noise %s, Delta %.2f, n_fits %d | ",
                     "ellipse emp/Fisher  k_off %.2f  N_ch %.2f | map  k_off %.2f  N_ch %.2f | ",
                     "area emp/Fisher %.2f, emp/corrected %.2f\n"),
              ALGO_LAB[[algo]], pt$lab, pt$what, pt$nch, pt$noise, pt$dlt, nf, rx, ry,
              mx, my, rF, rC))
  list(g = ggplot() +
    geom_point(data = pts, aes(off, Num_ch_mean), colour = "grey70", size = .2, alpha = .4) +
    geom_path(data = src, aes(x, y, colour = source, linetype = source, linewidth = source)) +
    geom_point(data = mk, aes(x, y, shape = kind), size = 1.7, stroke = .55, fill = "white") +
    scale_shape_manual(values = c(truth = 3, `empirical mean` = 16, `predicted (truth + bias)` = 21)) +
    annotate("label", x = gx + hx, y = gy + hy, hjust = 1.03, vjust = 1.15, size = 2.2,
             colour = bandcol(rx), fill = "white", label.size = 0,
             label.padding = unit(0.6, "pt"), parse = TRUE,
             label = sprintf("k[off]*\" %.2f×\"", rx)) +
    annotate("label", x = gx + hx, y = gy + hy, hjust = 1.03, vjust = 2.5, size = 2.2,
             colour = bandcol(ry), fill = "white", label.size = 0,
             label.padding = unit(0.6, "pt"), parse = TRUE,
             label = sprintf("N[ch]*\" %.2f×\"", ry)) +
    scale_colour_manual(values = c(empirical = "grey35", Fisher = "#D55E00", corrected = "#0072B2")) +
    scale_linetype_manual(values = c(empirical = "solid", Fisher = "dashed", corrected = "dotted")) +
    scale_linewidth_manual(values = c(empirical = .5, Fisher = .7, corrected = .85), guide = "none") +
    labs(x = expression(log[10]~k[off]), y = expression(log[10]~N[ch]),
         title = paste0(ALGO_LAB[[algo]], "   ·   ", pt$lab, "  ", pt$what),
         subtitle = bquote(N[ch] == .(pt$nch) ~ "," ~ sigma == .(pt$noise) ~ "," ~
                           Delta %.% k[off] == .(pt$dlt))) +
    theme_bw(base_size = 8, base_family = "Helvetica") +
    theme(panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
          plot.title = element_text(size = 7.5, hjust = .5, face = "bold", margin = margin(b = 1)),
          plot.subtitle = element_text(size = 6, hjust = .5, margin = margin(b = 2)),
          axis.text = element_text(size = 6), axis.title = element_text(size = 7.5),
          aspect.ratio = 1,
          legend.position = "bottom", legend.title = element_blank(), legend.box = "horizontal",
          legend.text = element_text(size = 6.5), legend.key.size = unit(.6, "lines"),
          legend.margin = margin(0, 0, 0, 0), legend.box.spacing = unit(2, "pt"),
          plot.margin = margin(2, 4, 2, 4)),
    gx = gx, gy = gy, hx = hx, hy = hy)
}

# build all six, then give the R and IR panels of a column the SAME window (the larger of the two).
# Without this the two clouds of a design point are drawn at different zooms and the visual
# comparison the block exists for is invalid.
cells6 <- expand_grid(algo = factor(ALGOS, levels = ALGOS), i = seq_len(nrow(ELL_POINTS)))
built  <- pmap(cells6, function(algo, i) panel(i, as.character(algo)))
gAlist <- lapply(seq_len(nrow(cells6)), function(k) {
  i    <- cells6$i[k]
  mate <- which(cells6$i == i)
  hx   <- max(vapply(built[mate], function(b) b$hx, numeric(1)))
  hy   <- max(vapply(built[mate], function(b) b$hy, numeric(1)))
  b    <- built[[k]]
  b$g + coord_cartesian(xlim = c(b$gx - hx, b$gx + hx), ylim = c(b$gy - hy, b$gy + hy))
})
gA <- wrap_plots(gAlist, nrow = length(ALGOS), byrow = TRUE, guides = "collect") &
        theme(legend.position = "bottom")
gA
```

```{r maps}
X_BRK <- log10(c(.01, .1, 1))
# y breaks come from the GRID, not hardcoded: the noise axis grows by three decades when the diagonal
# cells land, and a fixed set would silently stop labelling the new ones. Powers of ten only, thinned
# to at most five so the labels never collide in a 1.5 in panel.
Y_DEC <- sort(unique(as.numeric(GRID$z)))
Y_BRK <- log10(Y_DEC[seq(1, length(Y_DEC), by = max(1, ceiling(length(Y_DEC) / 5)))])
Y_LAB <- formatC(10^Y_BRK, format = "g", digits = 3)

# ONE row factor, not nested facets: levels ordered parameter-major, algorithm-minor, so the R/IR
# pair of a parameter is always adjacent. The strip is a two-line parsed label (parameter over
# algorithm) because a row label is not an axis label and will not fit inside one at this size.
rowfac <- function(d, params) {
  lv <- as.vector(t(outer(params, ALGOS,
          function(p, a) sprintf("atop(%s, bold(\"%s\"))", PARAM_MATH[p], ALGO_LAB[a]))))
  d %>% filter(param %in% params) %>%
    mutate(prow = factor(sprintf("atop(%s, bold(\"%s\"))", PARAM_MATH[param], ALGO_LAB[algo]),
                         levels = lv))
}

marks <- function(rows) ELL_POINTS %>%
  mutate(lx = log10(dlt), ly = log10(as.numeric(noise)),
         nchf = factor(paste0("N[ch]==", nch), levels = paste0("N[ch]==", NCHS))) %>%
  tidyr::crossing(prow = factor(rows, levels = rows))

mapblock <- function(d, zvar, pal, brk, params, top = FALSE, bottom = FALSE) {
  if ("comp" %in% names(d)) d <- filter(d, comp == "total")   # the maps show the total, not its parts
  d  <- rowfac(d, params) %>% mutate(zz = pmin(pmax(zvar(.data), min(brk)), max(brk)))
  mk <- marks(levels(d$prow))
  uni <- if ("unident" %in% names(d)) filter(d, unident) else d[0, ]
  ggplot(d, aes(lx, ly, z = zz)) +
    geom_contour_filled(breaks = brk) +
    scale_fill_manual(values = pal, drop = FALSE, guide = "none") +
    geom_point(size = .08, colour = "grey35", alpha = .35) +
    # grey out the unidentified corner (over the fill): value not meaningful there
    geom_tile(data = uni, aes(lx, ly), inherit.aes = FALSE, fill = "grey60",
              colour = "grey30", linewidth = .25, width = 0.30, height = 1.0) +
    geom_point(data = mk, aes(lx, ly), inherit.aes = FALSE, shape = 21, fill = "white",
               colour = "black", size = 1.5, stroke = .45) +
    geom_text(data = mk, aes(lx, ly, label = lab), inherit.aes = FALSE, size = 1.9,
              fontface = "bold", vjust = -0.95) +
    facet_grid(prow ~ nchf, labeller = label_parsed) +
    scale_x_continuous(breaks = X_BRK, labels = c(".01", ".1", "1")) +
    scale_y_continuous(breaks = Y_BRK, labels = Y_LAB) +
    labs(y = "noise") +
    theme_bw(base_size = 8, base_family = "Helvetica") +
    # GREY PANEL, not white. On a shared ragged axis a column is only filled up to the highest noise
    # it was run at, and the CI-shrink rule renders a CALIBRATED cell white. Against a white panel
    # those two are the same picture: "we measured it and it is fine" would be indistinguishable from
    # "we never ran it". Grey is the absence of data; white inside the filled region is calibration.
    theme(panel.background = element_rect(fill = "grey85", colour = NA),
          panel.grid.minor = element_blank(), panel.spacing = unit(0.12, "cm"),
          legend.position = "none", axis.text = element_text(size = 6),
          axis.title.y = element_text(size = 8), strip.text.y = element_text(size = 6.5, angle = 0),
          plot.margin = margin(2, 2, 2, 2)) +
    (if (top) theme(strip.text.x = element_text(size = 7.5)) else theme(strip.text.x = element_blank())) +
    (if (bottom) labs(x = expression(Delta %.% k[off])) else
       theme(axis.title.x = element_blank(), axis.text.x = element_blank()))
}

colorbar <- function(pal, edge_lab, title, lin, sol = numeric(0), dsh = numeric(0)) {
  n <- length(pal); pos <- function(v) match(v, lin) - 0.5
  mkx <- c(
    if (length(dsh)) list(annotate("segment", x = pos(dsh), xend = pos(dsh), y = 0.5, yend = 2.0,
                                   colour = "grey25", linewidth = .35, linetype = "dashed")),
    if (length(sol)) list(annotate("segment", x = pos(sol), xend = pos(sol), y = 0.5, yend = 2.0,
                                   colour = "black", linewidth = .5)))
  ggplot(data.frame(x = seq_len(n)), aes(x, 1, fill = factor(x))) +
    geom_tile(colour = "grey92", linewidth = .15) + mkx +
    scale_fill_manual(values = pal, guide = "none") +
    scale_x_continuous(breaks = seq(0.5, n + 0.5, 1), labels = edge_lab, expand = c(0, 0)) +
    coord_cartesian(ylim = c(0.5, 2.0), clip = "off") +
    labs(y = title) + theme_void(base_family = "Helvetica") +
    theme(axis.title.y = element_text(angle = 0, size = 7, hjust = 1, vjust = .5, margin = margin(r = 4)),
          axis.text.x = element_text(size = 5.5), axis.ticks.x = element_line(colour = "grey55", linewidth = .3),
          axis.ticks.length.x = unit(2, "pt"), plot.margin = margin(3, 26, 2, 34))
}

btitle <- function(txt) ggplot() + theme_void(base_family = "Helvetica") +
  annotate("text", x = 0, y = 0, label = txt, hjust = 0, size = 2.9, fontface = "bold") +
  coord_cartesian(xlim = c(0, 1), clip = "off") + theme(plot.margin = margin(3, 2, 0, 26))

# one assembler, a roster PER BLOCK. Block A is shared: an ellipse needs two axes, so it stays on the
# (k_off, N_ch) pair whatever the maps show.
#
# THE TWO MOMENTS LIVE IN DIFFERENT PARAMETERS, measured, not assumed: the bias is in N_ch (R 0.126 /
# 0.148 / 0.138 / 0.138 log10 across the four decades against IR's 0.004 / 0 / 0 / 0) while k_off's
# bias is ~0 for both (R -0.008 / -0.0007 / -0.0001 / 0). The distortion is the other way round: it is
# k_off that carries the flat 1.44 floor. So a short version cannot be made by dropping a parameter
# from both blocks - that empties one of them. It is made by giving each block the direction that
# carries ITS failure.
assemble <- function(pb, pd) {
  nrb <- length(pb) * length(ALGOS)             # map rows in the bias block
  nrd <- length(pd) * length(ALGOS)             # map rows in the distortion block
  gC <- mapblock(bias, function(d) d$Bconf,        BIAS_PAL, BIAS_LIN,    pb, top = TRUE)
  gB <- mapblock(stat, function(d) log10(d$Dconf), DIST_PAL, DIST_BREAKS, pd, bottom = TRUE)
  g <- btitle("A   grouped MLE distributions") /
       gA /
       btitle("B   distortion-induced bias") /
       gC /
       colorbar(BIAS_PAL, BIAS_LAB, "bias (log10 units)", BIAS_LIN) /
       btitle("C   information distortion") /
       gB /
       colorbar(DIST_PAL, DIST_LAB, "distortion", DIST_LIN, sol = c(0.87, 1.15), dsh = c(0.67, 1.5))
  # the bottom map block carries the x axis, so it gets a little more height per row than the top one
  hs <- c(0.16, 2.30 * length(ALGOS), 0.16, 0.90 * nrb, 0.28, 0.16, 0.95 * nrd, 0.28)
  list(g = g + plot_layout(heights = hs), h = sum(hs))
}
```

```{r render-short, fig.width = 7, fig.height = 9.4}
# SHORT: one direction per block, each the one that carries that block's failure. Bias on N[ch],
# distortion on k[off]. Two map rows per block, and neither of them is blank.
short <- assemble("Num_ch_mean", "off")
short$g
ggsave("Figure_4_short_variant.pdf", short$g, width = 7.0, height = short$h)
cat("Figure_4_short_variant.pdf: bias on N_ch, distortion on k_off; height", round(short$h, 2), "in\n")
```

```{r render-long, fig.width = 7, fig.height = 13.1}
# LONG: both directions in both blocks. The two parameters carry OPPOSITE signs - R's N_ch distortion
# crosses from conservative to over-confident across the columns while its k_off floor stays flat -
# which is why any scalar averaged over parameters cancels them and reports a faithful algorithm.
# Only this version makes that verifiable, and it is the one that shows the bias and the distortion
# are not in the same direction.
long <- assemble(PARAM_LONG, PARAM_LONG)
long$g
ggsave("Figure_4.pdf", long$g, width = 7.0, height = long$h)
cat("Figure_4.pdf: both directions in both blocks; height", round(long$h, 2), "in\n")
```

```{r caption-numbers}
# Every number the caption quotes, printed on each knit so none of them is ever read off the plot.
cat("\n=== Figure 4 (R vs IR) caption numbers ===\n")

cat("\nDISTORTION, median over the 7 intervals, by algorithm / parameter / N_ch (noise 0.1)\n")
print(as.data.frame(stat %>% filter(comp == "total", noise == "0.1") %>%
        group_by(param, algo, Num_ch) %>% summarise(med = median(Dconf), .groups = "drop") %>%
        mutate(algo = ALGO_LAB[algo]) %>%
        pivot_wider(names_from = Num_ch, values_from = med)), digits = 3, row.names = FALSE)

cat("\nDOES NOISE CURE IT? same medians at each noise level, k_off\n")
print(as.data.frame(stat %>% filter(comp == "total", param == "off") %>%
        group_by(algo, noise, Num_ch) %>% summarise(med = median(Dconf), .groups = "drop") %>%
        mutate(algo = ALGO_LAB[algo]) %>%
        pivot_wider(names_from = Num_ch, values_from = med) %>%
        arrange(algo, as.numeric(noise))), digits = 3, row.names = FALSE)

cat("\nBIAS (log10 units), median over the 7 intervals, noise 0.1\n")
print(as.data.frame(bias %>% filter(noise == "0.1") %>%
        group_by(param, algo, Num_ch) %>% summarise(med = median(Bconf), .groups = "drop") %>%
        mutate(algo = ALGO_LAB[algo]) %>%
        pivot_wider(names_from = Num_ch, values_from = med)), digits = 3, row.names = FALSE)

cat("\nEXTENT: fraction of (interval, noise) cells departing significantly\n")
cat("  distortion, |D-1| >= 0.15:\n")
print(as.data.frame(stat %>% filter(comp == "total") %>% group_by(param, algo, Num_ch) %>%
        summarise(f = mean(abs(Dconf - 1) >= 0.15), .groups = "drop") %>%
        mutate(algo = ALGO_LAB[algo]) %>%
        pivot_wider(names_from = Num_ch, values_from = f)), digits = 2, row.names = FALSE)
cat("  bias, interval excludes 0:\n")
print(as.data.frame(bias %>% group_by(param, algo, Num_ch) %>%
        summarise(f = mean(Bconf != 0), .groups = "drop") %>%
        mutate(algo = ALGO_LAB[algo]) %>%
        pivot_wider(names_from = Num_ch, values_from = f)), digits = 2, row.names = FALSE)
```
